Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents Pursuant To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Served Upon Third Party Fidelity National Title Company
Off Calendar Jun-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Leo M. Larocca, of Law Offices of Niven & Smith, LLP, Tel. 415.981.5451, for defendants D.D.P.M., Inc. and Josef Hersh;
Robert B. Hunter II, of Fidelity National Law Group, Tel. 925.817.3716, for Fidelity National Title Co., appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT JOSEF HERSH, D.D.P.M., INC. DBA ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE Motion To Compel Documents Pursuant To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Served Upon Third Party Fidelity National Title Company is granted. The third party objections are overruled and within twenty-five (25) days from the date of this order, Fidelity National Title Company will produce to defendant all documents responsive to the deposition requesst for the period September, October and November, 2016, as it pertains to California transactions. If Fidelity seeks to withhold any documents asserting a claim of attorney/client privilege it must prepare and deliver within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order to the moving party a privilege log identifying the documents and the basis for the claim of privilege with sufficient specificity to allow moving party to challenge said claim. The Court signs the protective order submitted by the moving party to protect against any claim of confidential or trade secret information. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
